CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 16122010) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC. ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 1.0 DAYS

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CANCELLATION OF AN INSTRUMENT, SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD, AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. THE FIRST CLAIM IS ADEQUATELY PLED BASED ON ALLEGED FORGERY. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE FRAUD WITH THE REQUIRED PARTICULARITY AND MR. PEREZ IS GIVEN LEAVE TO CORRECT THIS DEFICIENCY IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. MR. PEREZ HAS ACKNOWLEDGED THAT HIS THIRD CLAIM IS DEFICIENT AS A MATTER OF LAW AND NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS CASE. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUN-22-2016 CONTINUED TO AUG-24-2016 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR CROSS COMPLAINANT(S) TO BRING FIRST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT(S) AT ISSUE. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
REPLY IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 16201002) FILED BY PLAINTIFF CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE ALLEGATION IN PARAGRAPH 5 THAT MR. ZEUSS WAS THE AGENT OR EMPLOYEE OF CANON WHEN HE PREPARED A WRITING WITH CANON AS A PROPOSED SIGNATORY IS ADEQUATE TO WITHSTAND A DEMURRER CHALLENGING THE SUFFICIENCY OF AN AGENCY/EMPLOYMENT ALLEGATION. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GISELLE CASEY, CSR #8098, BUS. TEL: 415-717-9768. (D302)

Off Calendar Aug-05-2016 Continued To Oct-05-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

CROSS-DEFENDANT CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The allegation in paragraph 5 that Mr. Zeuss was the agent or employee of Canon when he prepared a writing with Canon as a proposed signatory is adequate to withstand a demurrer challenging the sufficiency of an agency/employment allegation. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098, Bus. Tel: 415-717-9768. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-07-2016 Continued To Aug-24-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT CANON FINANCIAL SERVICES INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT is overruled as to the first cause of action for cancellation of an instrument, sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for fraud, and sustained without leave to amend as to the third cause of action for unfair business practices. The first claim is adequately pled based on alleged forgery. The second cause of action fails to allege fraud with the required particularity and Mr. Perez is given leave to correct this deficiency if he can do so in good faith. Mr. Perez has acknowledged that his third claim is deficient as a matter of law and not applicable to this case. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
